tasks.question: A photo-sharing application was acquired by a major social network in April 2012 for approximately one billion dollars. In what year was this application originally launched?
A social networking company founded at Harvard University later acquired both Instagram and WhatsApp. In what year was this company itself founded?
A billionaire entrepreneur completed the acquisition of a major social media platform in October 2022, taking it private. How many billions of dollars was the acquisition price?
A famous painting stolen from a Paris museum in 1911 was recovered two years later. Who was the artist who created this masterpiece?
The highest mountain on Earth is located on the border between Nepal and Tibet. What is its height in feet?
A physicist who developed the theory of relativity and the equation E=mc² was born in a German city. What is the name of that city?
A famous ocean liner sank in 1912 after hitting an iceberg. Approximately how many people died in this disaster?

A social media giant acquired a photo-sharing app for around $1 billion and later acquired a messaging service for a much larger sum. How many billions of dollars did the messaging service acquisition cost?
The founder of a social network that was launched from a Harvard dormitory in 2004 was born in a city in New York state. What is the name of that city?
A scientist who discovered two elements (one named after her home country) won Nobel Prizes in two different fields. In what year did she win her second Nobel Prize?
A famous physicist who published E=mc² won the Nobel Prize, but not for relativity. For what discovery did he actually receive the Nobel Prize?
A British playwright born in Stratford-upon-Avon married a woman 8 years his senior when he was 18. What was his wife's first name?
The wreck of a famous ship that sank in 1912 was discovered by Robert Ballard decades later. In what year was the wreck found?
Two climbers made the first confirmed ascent of Earth's highest peak in 1953. One was from New Zealand. What was the first name of the Nepalese Sherpa who accompanied him?
A wall that divided a major European city fell in November 1989. How many years had the wall been standing before it fell?

A tech company made three major acquisitions: a photo app for $1 billion, a messaging app for $19 billion, and a VR company for $2 billion. What is the total amount spent on these three acquisitions in billions of dollars?

A messaging app co-founder who was born in Ukraine emigrated to the US as a teenager. The app he co-founded was later acquired by a company whose founder was born in New York state. What is the first name of the Ukrainian-born co-founder?

Two female scientists won Nobel Prizes in Chemistry - one in 2020 for gene editing technology, and one over a century earlier for discovering radioactive elements. How many years apart were these Nobel Prizes awarded?

The first person to walk on the Moon did so in 1969. A famous physicist who died in 1955 and whose brain was preserved never witnessed this achievement. How many years before the Moon landing did this physicist die?

A wall that divided a German city fell in 1989 after standing for 28 years. A famous ocean liner that sank in 1912 was discovered 4 years before the wall fell. What is the difference in years between when the wall was built and when the ship sank?

An AI company received investments from a tech giant totaling $11 billion ($1 billion in 2019, $10 billion in 2023). The same investor's co-founder also helped found this AI company in 2015. Who is the CEO of this AI company?
A scientist who won two Nobel Prizes was born in Warsaw in 1867 and died from radiation-related illness. She discovered an element named after her home country. How many years did she live?

A river that carries about 20% of Earth's freshwater flows through a rainforest that produces about 20% of Earth's oxygen. The river basin spans 9 countries. What percentage of the basin is in the largest country?
An inventor who created the alternating current motor and was honored by having both a unit of measurement and a car company named after him was born in what is now Croatia. In what year was he born?
An electric car company named after a famous inventor was actually founded by two different people before Elon Musk became CEO. In what year was this company founded?
An electric car company is named after an inventor who died in 1943. How many years after the inventor's death was the company that bears his name founded?
A company that started as an online bookstore in 1994 later expanded to sell everything and acquired a grocery chain. Who founded this company?

Two major rivers are often debated as the world's longest. One flows through Egypt and is approximately 4,130 miles. The other flows through Brazil and carries 20% of Earth's freshwater. Which river is generally considered longer?
A planet named after the Roman god of war has the largest volcano in our solar system. How tall is this volcano in feet?
One entrepreneur named his electric car company after an inventor who died in New York, and also plans to send humans to Mars. In what year does he target for the first crewed Mars mission?
A Spanish artist who co-founded Cubism and painted Guernica created thousands of artworks. Approximately how many artworks did he create during his lifetime?
A Spanish artist who died in France in 1973 lived longer than a British playwright who died in 1616. How many years did the Spanish artist live?
A global conflict began when Germany invaded Poland and ended after atomic bombs were dropped on Japan. On what date did Germany invade Poland?
A wall dividing a German city was built 16 years after World War II ended. Germany had surrendered on May 8, 1945. In what month and year was this wall constructed?
An ancient defensive structure built primarily during the Ming Dynasty is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Approximately how many miles long is it including all branches?

A German wall stood for 28 years before falling in 1989. An ancient Chinese wall built over centuries stretches over 13,000 miles. The German wall fell how many years after the Chinese wall became a UNESCO World Heritage Site (1987)?
